dosing_enrichment.titration_note: Begin at 1.5 mg SC 2x/week for weeks 1-4 to establish baseline immune activation. Maintain through week 12. In high-burden states, daily dosing at 1.5 mg/day for weeks 1-4 under physician supervision.
dosing_enrichment.cycling_recommendation: 12-week active protocol followed by 6-8 week off-cycle assessment. Repeat cycle if NK cell counts and immune biomarkers remain suboptimal. Continuous use not advised beyond 16 weeks without biomarker re-evaluation.
dosing_enrichment.maintenance_dose: Thymosin Alpha-1: 1.5 mg SC 2x/week (standard). Option for 3x/week in immune-compromised patients or post-infectious recovery.
dosing_enrichment.timing_optimization: Administer in the morning, ideally post-fasting. Subcutaneous injection into the abdomen or thigh. Rotate injection sites.

protocol_author_notes: Official curated protocol for general clinical use.

qualitative: Reduced frequency and severity of upper respiratory infections
Enhanced natural killer (NK) cell cytotoxic activity
Improved vaccine response in immunocompromised individuals
Reduced systemic inflammatory cytokine burden (IL-6, TNF-alpha)
Accelerated recovery after illness or immune stress events

quantitative_ranges.week_8: IL-6 and TNF-alpha reduction of 25-40% in inflammatory responders; HBV/HCV response improvement in viral hepatitis adjunct use (Mutchnick 1991)
quantitative_ranges.week_4: NK cell activity increases 20-35% from baseline; CD4+ T-cell count rise measurable in immunocompromised subjects (Ershler 1989)
quantitative_ranges.week_12: Sustained NK activation in ~75% of responders; infection frequency reduction of 40-60% in immune-deficient populations (Romani 2012)

expected_outcomes.responder_rate_pct: 70-80% for NK cell enhancement; 60-70% for infection frequency reduction

overview_summary: Dual-thymosin immune modulation protocol combining Thymosin Alpha-1 and TB-500 to support T-cell function, innate immune calibration, tissue repair, and inflammation control.
protocol_title: Thymosin Alpha-1 & TB-500 Immune Modulation Protocol

legacy_compatibility.notes: Enhanced protocol preserves legacy fields while supporting canonical blueprint-based loading.

monitoringSchedule.label: End-of-Protocol Evaluation
tests: Full inflammatory panel: CRP, ESR, IL-6, TNF-α
monitoringSchedule.notes: Full outcome assessment and decision on continuation or cycling.

side_effects.management: Rotate sites; use proper aseptic technique.
Reduce dose; monitor blood pressure; salt restriction.
Dose with food; split doses; titrate slowly.
Monitor fasting glucose weekly; consult if persistent.
Reduce dose; psychological support if needed.

escalation_criteria: Severe or worsening edema — STOP, cardiovascular evaluation
Fasting glucose > 7.0 mmol/L (126 mg/dL) on two occasions — Endocrinology referral
Hypertensive urgency (BP > 180/110) — STOP, immediate medical attention
riskManagement.storage_handling: Store peptides at 2–8°C (refrigerated). Reconstituted vials: stable for 28 days refrigerated. Do not freeze reconstituted product. Protect from light.
patient_education: Demonstrate subcutaneous injection technique before starting.
Provide written dosing schedule and injection site rotation map.
Instruct patient to report any unusual symptoms within 24h.
Provide emergency contact number for out-of-hours concerns.

clinical_disclaimer: For use by qualified healthcare professionals only. Not a diagnosis, prescription, or substitute for individualized medical judgment, informed consent, and local regulatory compliance.

evidence_limitations: Several peptide uses in this library remain off-label, investigational, exploratory, or based on translational evidence.
Protocol data should not be interpreted as individualized medical advice or a substitute for prescribing clinician judgment.
Regulatory status varies by country and must be checked locally before clinical use or commercial distribution.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.update_scope: missing clinical/regulatory/safety information added while preserving canonical antigravity_v2 data model
latest_clinical_notes: Thymosin alpha-1 has international clinical use in several countries but is not broadly FDA-approved as a US drug product.
TB-500/thymosin beta-4 use remains investigational; emphasize autoimmune flare and malignancy-history caution.
Immune protocols should document infection history, autoimmune history, CBC with differential, CRP, and liver function where relevant.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_guardrail: Use under qualified medical supervision where legally permitted; obtain informed consent for off-label or investigational use.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.regulatory_status: Thymosin Alpha-1 has international clinical use/approvals in several countries for immune-related indications but is not FDA-approved in the United States. TB-500, KPV, thymulin and related peptide uses remain investigational/compounded/research depending on jurisdiction.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.evidence_update: Immune protocols should retain physician supervision and autoimmune/malignancy cautions.
clinical_evidence_update_2026.clinical_use_note: This protocol is informational and designed for clinician-supervised review. It is not a standalone prescription, diagnosis, or patient-specific treatment plan.

metadata.longDescription: Thymosin Alpha-1 (Tα1; Zadaxin) is a 28-amino acid peptide endogenously produced by thymic epithelial cells and the first thymic hormone to be fully sequenced. It promotes T-cell differentiation and maturation, augments NK cell activity, stimulates dendritic cell function, activates Toll-like receptors 2 and 9 (innate immunity), and modulates cytokine balance toward Th1-type responses (Goldstein AL & Goldstein A, Expert Opin Biol Ther 2009; King R & Tuthill C, Prog Mol Biol Transl Sci 2014). Tα1 is clinically approved in 35+ countries for hepatitis B, hepatitis C, and oncology supportive care. TB-500 (synthetic Thymosin β4 fragment) provides complementary immune modulation by suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-1β, TNF-α), promoting regulatory T-cell function, and accelerating tissue repair via angiogenesis and actin remodeling (Goldstein AL et al., Ann N Y Acad Sci 2012). The 8-week dual-phase protocol delivers combined immunomodulation in weeks 1–4, transitioning to Tα1 maintenance for sustained immune calibration.

references.citation: Goldstein AL, Goldstein A. From lab to bedside: emerging clinical applications of thymosin alpha 1. Expert Opin Biol Ther 2009;9:593-608.
King R, Tuthill C. Immune Modulation with Thymosin Alpha 1 Treatment. Prog Mol Biol Transl Sci 2014;129:179-208.
Goldstein AL et al. Thymosin β4: A multi-functional regenerative peptide. Ann N Y Acad Sci 2012;1269:1-8.

metadata.scientificName: Thymosin Alpha-1 & Thymosin β4 (TB-500) Thymic T-Cell & Innate Immune Modulation Protocol
metadata.description: 8-week dual-thymosin immunomodulation protocol combining Thymosin Alpha-1 T-cell maturation and toll-like receptor activation with TB-500 anti-inflammatory angiogenesis for comprehensive immune recalibration.
metadata.clinical_summary: Thymosin Alpha-1 (Tα1) is a thymic peptide that activates dendritic cells and T-cell maturation via Toll-like receptor 9 signalling, increasing TH1 cytokine output (IL-12, IFN-γ) and NK cell activity. TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4 fragment) reduces NF-κB-driven systemic inflammation, promotes angiogenesis and tissue repair, and modulates actin cytoskeleton dynamics in immune and stromal cells. Together they provide complementary immune restoration (Tα1) and anti-inflammatory tissue support (TB-500).
metadata.source_quality_note: Clinical content consolidated from protocol library and public biomedical literature; final medical use requires qualified clinician review.
metadata.regulatory_note: Updated 2026: Thymosin alpha-1 is approved/used in selected non-US markets for immune and viral-hepatitis-related indications, but is not broadly FDA-approved in the United States. TB-500/thymosin beta-4 remains investigational for clinical immune optimization.

scheduled_checkpoints.purpose: Assess early tolerability and inflammatory trend.
Evaluate immune activation and response signal.
Determine whether repeat cycle or washout is appropriate.

generator_rules.notes: Default generator rules – pending clinical review.

drug_interactions: Avoid concurrent immunosuppressants (corticosteroids, tacrolimus) - antagonistic on T-cell activation
Synergistic with antivirals in HBV/HCV adjunct protocols
Potential additive effect with other thymosin-class peptides - avoid stacking without oversight
monitoring_required: NK cell panel (CD16+/CD56+) at baseline and week 8
CRP and IL-6 if inflammatory reduction is primary goal
Liver function tests if used as viral hepatitis adjunct
adverse_events_serious: No serious adverse events in published trials at 1.5 mg doses
Theoretical risk of immune dysregulation in active autoimmune disease
adverse_events_common: Mild injection site reactions: redness, swelling (10-15%)
Transient flu-like symptoms in first 1-2 weeks (5-10%)
Mild headache during initial immune activation phase
